schemas.name: Company Share Transfer
schemas.description: Company Share Transfer by CA/CS firm. Companies Act compliance. Expert filing and advisory. Virtual Auditor.

step.text: Issue new share certificates within 30 days
step.name: Step 5

mainEntity.name: Is stamp duty payable on share transfer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Stamp duty on share transfer varies by state — typically 0.015% to 0.25% of consideration. Off-market transfers of dematerialised shares: 0.015%.

mainEntity.name: What if the transfer is to a foreigner?

acceptedAnswer.text: FEMA compliance required: DCF valuation, pricing at or below ceiling (for outbound), FC-TRS filing within 60 days.

mainEntity.name: How to transfer shares of a private limited company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Execute share transfer deed (SH-4). Board passes transfer resolution. Company issues new share certificates within 30 days. Stamp duty: 0.015% of consideration or market value. File MGT-14 if Articles restrict transfer.

mainEntity.name: Is stamp duty applicable on share transfers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. 0.015% (15 paisa per ₹100) of consideration or market value, whichever is higher. Payable by the transferee. For demat shares, stamp duty is collected at the depository level.

Private companies can restrict share transfer through Articles of Association. Common restrictions: board approval, right of first refusal to existing shareholders, lock-in periods. Board cannot unreasonably refuse transfer.

mainEntity.name: Is valuation needed for share transfer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Required when transfer is between related parties, for FEMA compliance (resident to non-resident or vice versa), and for Income Tax purposes (Section 56(2)(x) — transfer below fair market value). IBBI Registered Valuer recommended.

mainEntity.name: How is fair market value determined for share transfer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rule 11UA of Income Tax Rules: DCF method or NAV method for unlisted companies. For FEMA: internationally accepted pricing methodology (usually DCF). We provide multi-framework valuation covering both requirements simultaneously.
